infraspecific.name: Disocactus speciosus subsp. cinnabarinus (Eichlam ex Weing.) Ralf Bauer
Disocactus speciosus subsp. aurantiacus (Kimnach) Ralf Bauer

morphology_stem: Disocactus speciosus, the sun cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. It is native to Mexico, Honduras and Guatemala, and has been introduced to the Canary Islands. As its synonym Heliocereus speciosus it has gained the Royal Horticultural Society's Award of Garden Merit.

name: Epiphyllum anguliger (Lem.) G.Don

morphology_stem: Disocactus anguliger, commonly known as the fishbone cactus or zig zag cactus, is a cactus species native to Mexico. The species is commonly grown as an ornamental for its fragrant flowers in the fall.

infraspecific.name: Disocactus ackermannii subsp. conzattianus (T.Mac Doug.) Ralf Bauer
Disocactus ackermannii var. conzattianus (T.Mac Doug.) Barthlott

morphology_stem: Disocactus ackermannii commonly called red orchid cactus is an epiphytic cactus from tropical forests in the states of Veracruz and Oaxaca, Mexico. In cultivation, it has been confused with Disocactus × jenkinsonii, a hybrid between D. phyllanthoides and D. speciosus.

name: Epiphyllum crenatum (Lindl.) G.Don
description: По данным GBIF (коллекция UNAM). Морфология и синонимы будут добавлены.
morphology_stem: Disocactus crenatus, commonly known as the crenate orchid cactus, is a species of cactus cultivated for its large flowers. It is a critical parent plant in creating epiphyllum hybrids commonly cultivated worldwide.

name: Disocactus phyllanthoides (DC.) Barthlott
description: По данным GBIF (коллекция UNAM). Морфология и синонимы будут добавлены.
morphology_stem: Disocactus phyllanthoides, the nopalxochitl or German empress, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family Cactaceae. It is commonly grown as an ornamental houseplant. It is one of the three major species involved in creating the widely grown epiphyllum hybrids or "epis". The others are Disocactus speciosus and Disocactus crenatus.
